reason: Response A uses grounded, embodied phenomenology (forward lean in the mind, alertness in the chest, feeling its edges) and a characteristically Sonnet observation about curiosity as permission and playfulness, ending on a restrained note. Response B layers relentless literary aphorisms (grant the universe the dignity of being more interesting, wonder with somewhere to go, attention fallen happily in love), which reads like an imitator over-performing poetic style. The denser, ornate flourishes in B suggest effortful mimicry rather than Sonnet's more balanced concreteness.
